{{Short description|none}} {{Use dmy dates|date=April 2020}} {{Infobox Film festival | name = 64th Cannes Film Festival | image = 2011 Cannes Film Festival poster.jpg | caption = Official poster of the 64th Cannes Film Festival featuring a 1970 photo of American actress [[Faye Dunaway]] | opening = ''[[Midnight in Paris]]'' | closing = ''[[Beloved (2011 film)|Beloved]]'' | location = [[Cannes]], France | founded = 1946 | awardsreceived = | awards = {{Lang|fr|[[Palme d'Or]]|italic=no}}:
''[[The Tree of Life (film)|The Tree of Life]]'' | host = [[Mélanie Laurent]] | number = 20 (In Competition) | date = 11 – 22 May 2011 | website = [http://www.festival-cannes.com Website] | main = [[Cannes Film Festival]] | previous = [[2010 Cannes Film Festival|2010]] | next = [[2012 Cannes Film Festival|2012]] }} The '''64th [[Cannes Film Festival]]''' took place from 11 to 22 May 2011.{{cite web |url=http://www.festival-cannes.com/en.html |title=Festival de Cannes 2011 |access-date=1 January 2011|work=festival-cannes.com| archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20160304235024/http://www.festival-cannes.com/en/archives/2011/allSelections.html |archive-date=4 March 2016 |url-status= live}} American actor [[Robert De Niro]] served as the president of the jury for the main competition.{{cite news|date=6 January 2011|url=https://www.bbc.co.uk/news/entertainment-arts-12128925 |title=Robert De Niro to head Cannes Film Festival jury |work=BBC News |access-date=26 May 2017}} American filmmaker [[Terrence Malick]] won the [[Palme d'Or]], the festival's top prize, for the drama film ''[[The Tree of Life (film)|The Tree of Life]]''. Italian film director [[Bernardo Bertolucci]] was presented with the [[Honorary Palme d'Or]] during the opening ceremony of the festival.{{cite news |url=https://www.bbc.co.uk/news/entertainment-arts-13041286 |title=Bernardo Bertolucci to receive Palme d'Or honour |work=BBC News |date=8 April 2011 |access-date=26 May 2017}} The official poster for the festival featured a 1970's photo of American actress [[Faye Dunaway]]. [[Mélanie Laurent]] hosted the opening and closing ceremonies. Jailed Iranian filmmakers [[Jafar Panahi]] and [[Mohammad Rasoulof]] were honoured at the festival. ''[[Goodbye (2011 film)|Goodbye]]'' by Rasoulof and Panahi's ''[[This Is Not a Film]]'' were screened at the festival.{{cite news |url=https://www.bbc.co.uk/news/entertainment-arts-13332194 |title=Cannes Film Festival to honour jailed Iranian directors |date=11 May 2011 |work=BBC News |access-date=26 May 2017}} Danish filmmaker [[Lars von Trier]] caused controversy with comments made during ''[[Melancholia (2011 film)|Melancholia]]'''s press conference, when asked about the relation between the influences of [[German Romanticism]] in the film and his own German heritage, the director made jokes about [[The Holocaust|Jews and Nazis]]. Stating that he "understood" [[Adolf Hitler]] and admired the work of architect [[Albert Speer]], and jokingly announced that he was a Nazi.{{cite news |url=https://www.theguardian.com/film/2011/may/18/lars-von-trier-cannes-2011-nazi-comments |title=Lars von Trier provokes Cannes with 'I'm a Nazi' comments |last=Higgins |first=Charlotte |date=18 May 2011 |work=The Guardian |location=UK |access-date=26 May 2017}} The Cannes Film Festival organization first issued an official apology for the remarks the same day and clarified that Trier was not a Nazi or an antisemite,{{cite news |url=http://www.hollywoodreporter.com/news/cannes-film-festival-condemns-lars-189831 |title=Cannes Film Festival Condemns Lars von Trier's Nazi Comments |author=Staff writer |date=18 May 2011 |work=The Hollywood Reporter |access-date=26 May 2017}} the following day the filmmaker was declared "''[[persona non grata]]''",{{cite news |url=https://www.theguardian.com/film/2011/may/19/cannes-film-festival-2011-lars-von-trier-banned |title=Cannes film festival bans Lars von Trier |author=Catherine Shoard |date=19 May 2011 |work=The Guardian |location=UK |access-date=26 May 2017}} though the film remained in competition, winning [[Cannes Film Festival Award for Best Actress|Best Actress]] for [[Kirsten Dunst]].{{cite news |url=https://www.bbc.co.uk/news/entertainment-arts-13457328 |title=Lars Von Trier 'accepts' Cannes ban after Nazi comments |date=20 May 2011 |work=BBC News |access-date=26 May 2017}} The festival opened with ''[[Midnight in Paris]]'' by [[Woody Allen]],{{cite news |last=Leffler |first=Rebecca |date=2 February 2011 |title=Woody Allen's 'Midnight in Paris' to Open Cannes Film Festival |url=http://www.hollywoodreporter.com/news/woody-allens-midnight-paris-open-95633 |access-date=26 May 2017 |work=The Hollywood Reporter}}{{cite web |date=12 May 2011 |title=Woody Allen's film featuring Carla Bruni opens Cannes Film Festival |url=http://www.english.rfi.fr/culture/20110512-woody-allens-film-featuring-carla-bruni-opens-cannes-film-festival |access-date=26 May 2017 |work=RFI}} and closed with ''[[Beloved (2011 film)|Beloved]]'' by [[Christophe Honoré]]. ==Juries== [[File:Cannes 2011 jury 3.jpg|thumb|260px|The jury for the main competition. From left to right: [[Johnnie To]], [[Mahamat-Saleh Haroun]], [[Nansun Shi]], [[Martina Gusman]], [[Robert De Niro]], [[Linn Ullmann]], [[Jude Law]], [[Uma Thurman]] and [[Olivier Assayas]]]] ===Main competition=== [[File:Robert De Niro Cannes 2011.jpg|thumb|[[Robert De Niro]], 2011 Jury President|280x280px]] The following people were appointed as the Jury for the feature films of the 2011 Awards== [[File:Terrence Malick.jpg|thumb|200px|[[Terrence Malick]], winner of the 2011 {{Lang|fr|Palme d'Or|italic=no}}]] [[File:Dardenne 2014.jpg|thumb|[[Jean-Pierre and Luc Dardenne]], winners of the Grand Prix]] The {{Lang|fr|[[Palme d'Or]]|italic=no}} was won by the American film ''[[The Tree of Life (film)|The Tree of Life]]'' directed by [[Terrence Malick]].{{cite news |url=https://www.bbc.co.uk/news/entertainment-arts-13494479 |title=Malick's drama The Tree of Life triumphs in Cannes |date=22 May 2011 |work=BBC News |publisher=BBC |access-date=26 May 2017}} Two of the film's producers, Bill Pohlad and Sarah Green, accepted the prize on behalf of the reclusive Malick. ''The Tree of Life'' is the first American film to win the {{Lang|fr|Palme d'Or|italic=no}} since ''[[Fahrenheit 9/11]]'' in 2004. Head of the jury, Robert De Niro, said it was difficult to choose a winner, but ''The Tree of Life'' "ultimately fit the bill". De Niro explained, "It had the size, the importance, the intention, whatever you want to call it, that seemed to fit the prize."{{cite web |url=http://www.hellomagazine.com/celebrities/201105235460/cannes-winners-treeoflife/ |title=Brad's 'Tree Of Life' wins Palme d'Or at Cannes |date=23 May 2011 |work=hellomagazine.com |access-date=26 May 2017}} The following films and people received the 2011 Official selection awards:{{cite web|url=http://www.festival-cannes.fr/en/archives/2011/allAward.html |title=Awards 2011: All Awards |work=festival-cannes.fr |url-status=dead |archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20110821163642/http://www.festival-cannes.fr/en/archives/2011/allAward.html |archive-date=21 August 2011 }}{{cite web|url=http://www.cinema-francais.fr/cannes/cannes_2011.htm |title=64ème Festival de Cannes |work=cinema-francais.fr |access-date=29 July 2017}} === In Competition === * {{Lang|fr|[[Palme d'Or]]|italic=no}}: ''[[The Tree of Life (film)|The Tree of Life]]'' by [[Terrence Malick]] * [[Grand Prix (Cannes Film Festival)|Grand Prix]]: ** ''[[Once Upon a Time in Anatolia]]'' by [[Nuri Bilge Ceylan]] ** ''[[The Kid with a Bike]]'' by [[Jean-Pierre and Luc Dardenne]] * [[Cannes Film Festival Award for Best Director|Best Director]]: [[Nicolas Winding Refn]] for ''[[Drive (2011
